title: Toe pain - NHS
text:
Urgent advice: Contact NHS 111 if:
you have badly hurt your big toe you're in severe pain you feel faint, dizzy or sick from the pain you have any tingling or loss of sensation in your foot your toe is pointing at an odd angle you heard a snap, grinding or popping noise at the time of injury you have difficulty moving your toes or you cannot walk you have toe pain and feel hot, cold or shivery These might be signs of a badly broken toe after an injury.
You can call 111 or get help from 111 online .
Information: 
Self-refer to a podiatrist If you have toe pain, you might be able to refer yourself directly to a podiatrist without seeing a GP.
To find out if there are any services in your area:
ask the reception staff at your GP surgery check your GP surgery's website contact your integrated care board (ICB) – find your local ICB search online for NHS podiatrists near you
